description_long: This object is a solar energy infrastructure installation featuring a single large photovoltaic panel mounted on a metal support frame. The panel is elevated above a rectangular base platform, which appears to simulate a section of a flat urban rooftop with various surface textures and colors, including gray, black, and yellow areas. The support structure consists of angled metal legs, providing stability and optimal tilt for solar exposure. The base includes several small rooftop fixtures, such as vents and utility boxes, suggesting integration with building infrastructure. The solar panel itself is composed of a grid of dark photovoltaic cells, framed by a metallic border. The overall design is utilitarian, with exposed structural and functional elements typical of urban solar installations.
description: A rooftop solar energy installation with a single large photovoltaic panel mounted on a metal frame above a detailed urban rooftop base, featuring vents and utility boxes.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the solar panel and the rooftop base with visible fixtures and surface details.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the panel's tilt and the depth of the rooftop base, with text indicating a rearward slope.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the solar panel, support structure, and rooftop features from the opposite side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizing the panel's tilt and the arrangement of rooftop elements beneath the panel.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 30 degrees, azimuth direction not clearly specified, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: roof-mounted on a flat platform with angled aluminum or steel frame supports
attribute_array_configuration: single row of 1 large panel centrally mounted above the base
